fact: January 23 is exactly 33 days away from the first day of spring in the Northern Hemisphere - that's like counting down a whole month plus three extra days!
In Antarctica right now, the sun never sets! Penguins are living in 24-hour daylight while you're bundled up in winter coats.
Tree sap is typically frozen solid in most cold places today, which means if you tapped a maple tree, you'd get ice crystals instead of syrup!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier today than it did last week in northern climates, even though winter feels like it's dragging on forever.
Aquarius season just started! People born today are supposedly the most rebellious and quirky of all zodiac signs.
In the Southern Hemisphere, cicadas are buzzing so loudly right now that they can drown out normal conversation - it's their peak summer party time!
Snow crystals forming today have six sides because of how water molecules freeze, but no two snowflakes will ever be exactly identical.
Hibernating bears in northern regions have been asleep for about 2 months now and won't wake up for another 2 months - they're basically living the ultimate winter nap!
January 23 falls on the 23rd day of the year, which means there are exactly 342 days left until next New Year's Eve (or 343 in leap years).
Ocean waves are usually bigger and more powerful during winter months like right now because of stronger storm systems churning up the water.
The moon often looks extra bright on clear January nights because cold air holds less moisture, making the atmosphere cleaner and more transparent.
Evergreen trees are working overtime today - they're the only plants still making oxygen while most other plants are taking a winter break in northern climates!
